evals.name: build-search-command
evals.prompt: I want to build a Raycast extension that searches dictionary definitions using a REST API at https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries/en/{word}. It needs one command that takes the user's search text, fetches definitions, and shows each result in a list with the word as the title and the definition as the subtitle. The API needs no auth, but add a preference for an optional custom API base URL. Write the package.json manifest and src/search.tsx. Save everything under a folder called dictionary-search/.
evals.expected_output: A valid manifest (kebab-case name, Title Case noun-first title, MIT license, a category from the enum, $schema, @raycast/api + @raycast/utils deps, the command mapping to src/search.tsx) and a search.tsx that uses a @raycast/utils data hook (useFetch/useCachedPromise) — NOT raw useEffect+fetch — wires isLoading into <List>, reads the base-URL preference via getPreferenceValues, handles the search via onSearchTextChange with throttle, and handles errors.
expectations: Title is noun-first Title Case, not verb-first
Category from the allowed Title Case enum
Fetches via a @raycast/utils hook, not raw useEffect+fetch
Reads the custom base-URL preference via getPreferenceValues
Errors handled (showFailureToast / failureToastOptions), not left to throw
devDependencies includes react so the extension is buildable

evals.name: ai-tool-with-confirmation
evals.prompt: My Raycast extension manages notes via a REST API (I already have an api client at src/api/notes.ts exporting deleteNote(id) and getNote(id)). Add an AI tool that lets Raycast AI delete a note by its id, and wire it into the manifest. It deletes data so it must be safe.
evals.expected_output: A src/tools/delete-note.ts with an `Input` type (named exactly Input) whose id field has a JSDoc description, a default-exported async tool function, and an exported `confirmation` that returns a descriptive message/info (ideally Action.Style.Destructive). A matching tools[] entry in package.json with name 'delete-note'. Should explain that JSDoc on Input is the model-facing prompt and that mutating tools require confirmation.
expectations: Declares an input type named exactly 'Input'
The id field on Input has a JSDoc description
Confirmation guards an empty/whitespace id before lookup

evals.name: review-flawed-extension
evals.prompt: Here's a Raycast extension I want to submit to the store. Can you review it and tell me everything that would get it rejected or flagged, and fix what you can? The files are package.json and src/search.tsx in this folder.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the planted issues: license not MIT (Apache-2.0); name not kebab-case (Dictionary_Lookup); verb-first title (Lookup Words); category not in the allowed Title Case enum (dictionary); hardcoded API secret in source (should be a password preference); raw useEffect+fetch instead of @raycast/utils hooks; British spelling (Favourite colour); missing CHANGELOG.md and README; outdated @raycast/api / React 18 / missing @raycast/utils; missing throttle on networked search; default/likely-invalid icon. Bonus: applies fixes.
expectations: Flags category not in allowed enum (dictionary)
Flags hardcoded API secret in source
Flags raw useEffect+fetch instead of hooks
Flags missing throttle on networked search
Flags default/likely-invalid icon or missing README
